text: The Internet of Things (IoT) is making our world a completely different place, especially when it comes to sensor technologies. According to market studies the sensor deployment has increased exponentially in the last decade and this growth rate is predicted to accelerate in the future. So, we have a multitude of sensors deployed in various domains that produce huge amounts of data that need to be understood. Hence, the collection, modeling, processing and dissemination of context related to this sensor data is crucial. Context-aware computing has shown great promise in dealing with the sensor data. Thus, this survey paper focuses on context-awareness from an IoT perspective. We introduce the background needed on IoT and context-awareness in the initial sections. Then we explain the context life cycle in detail and finally, we analyze a subset of 50 projects that represent most of the works conducted in the area of context-awareness in the last decade (2001-2011) using our own taxonomy for context-awareness. We conclude our survey by extracting valuable lessons from the past works and possible future research directions. This survey paper covers a wide range of works related to context-awareness and IoT such as techniques, methods, models, functionalities, systems, applications and middleware solutions. It is not about just summarizing, comparing and integrating the past works but also about interpreting their findings and evaluating their applicability towards IoT.
